name: isodesign

metadata.name: isodesign
metadata.summary: Facilitates the choice of the optimal isotopic composition of labeled substrates in 13C-fluxomics experiments.

documentation:
# IsoDesign

[![PyPI version](https://badge.fury.io/py/isodesign.svg)](https://badge.fury.io/py/isodesign)
[![Documentation Status](https://readthedocs.org/projects/isodesign/badge/?version=latest)](https://isodesign.readthedocs.io/en/latest/?badge=latest)

## What is IsoDesign ?
IsoDesign is a scientific tool designed to optimize the choice of the optimal 
isotopic composition of labeled substrates in 13C fluxomic labelling experiments. Simulated labeling is calculated using the [*influx_si* software](https://influx-si.readthedocs.io/en/latest/index.html), distributed with IsoDesign. 

It is one of the routine tools that we use at the [Mathematics cell of TBI](https://www.toulouse-biotechnology-institute.fr/en/plateformes-plateaux/cellule-mathematiques/) 
and [MetaToul platform](https://www.metabohub.fr/home.html).

The code is open-source, and available under a GPLv3 license.

Detailed documentation can be found online at Read the Docs ([https://isodesign.readthedocs.io/](https://isodesign.readthedocs.io/)).

## Key features

   * user can **define lower and upper bounds** (between 0 and 100) and **define the desired number of intervals** for each isotopic form to be included,
   * **substrate pricing can be entered**, which will be incorporated into the scoring criteria,
   * **simulation of all possible combinations** of isotopic forms in a single run,
   * ability to **simulate stationary or instationary labeling**, 
   * **multiple scoring criteria** are available and can be applied simultaneously to assess simulation results,
   * the results of the scoring criteria are displayed using a table for detailed values and a bar plot for visual representation,
   * shipped as a **library** with a **graphical** interface,
   * **open-source, free and easy to install** everywhere where Python 3 and pip run,
   * **biologist-friendly**.


## Quick-start

IsoDesign requires Python 3.10 or higher and run on all platforms.
Please check [the documentation](https://isodesign.readthedocs.io/en/latest/quickstart.html) for complete
installation and usage instructions.

### Installation with pip
If you have Python3 and R 
installed on your system, you can install IsoDesign from Pypi with pip :

```bash
$ pip install isodesign
```

To use IsoDesign, you will need to some R dependencies (necessary for the simulation part with influx_si).
Once you installed IsoDesign, you can install these dependencies by running the following command:

```bash
$ influx_s --install_rdep
```

For more information on the installation of R dependencies, please refer to [influx_si software](https://influx-si.readthedocs.io/en/latest/install.html#r-dependencies) documentation.


Then, start the graphical interface with:

```bash
$ isodesign
```

IsoDesign is also available directly from command-line and as a Python library.


## Bug and feature requests
If you have an idea on how we could improve IsoDesign please submit a new *issue*
to [our GitHub issue tracker](https://github.com/MetaboHUB-MetaToul-FluxoMet/IsoDesign/issues).


## Developers guide
### Contributions
Contributions are very welcome! :heart:

Please work on your own fork,
follow [PEP8](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0008/) style guide,
and make sure you pass all the tests before a pull request.

### Local install with pip
In development mode, do a `pip install -e /path/to/isodesign` to install
locally the development version.

### Build the documentation locally
Build the HTML documentation with:

```bash
$ cd doc
$ make html
```

The PDF documentation can be built locally by replacing `html` by `latexpdf`
in the command above. You will need a recent latex installation.

## Authors
Rochelle KOUAKOU, Loic LE GREGAM

## Contacts
:email: kouakou@insa-toulouse.fr, legregam@insa-toulouse.fr

methods.name: isodesign.base.label_input.LabelInput.__init__
documentation:
Class for the generation of all possible combinations of labelled substrates

```
param isotopomer_group

dictionary containing as key substrate name of the isotopomer group
and as values a list of isotopomer
```

methods.name: isodesign.base.label_input.LabelInput.generate_labelling_combinations
documentation: Generate all possible combinations of label input proportions for all isotopomers group

methods.name: isodesign.base.label_input.LabelInput._check_labelling_combinations
documentation: Check if the sum of all fractions is equal to 1 for all isotopomers group

modules.name: isodesign.tests.conftest
documentation: Configuration variables for the tests

classes.name: isodesign.base.isotopomer.Isotopomer

methods.name: isodesign.base.isotopomer.Isotopomer.__init__
documentation:
---
param intervals_nb: number of intervals for proportions to test
param labelling: labelling for isotopomer. 1 denotes heavy isotopes while 0 denotes
  natural isotope.
param lower_bound: minimum proportion to test
param name: Isotopomer name
param price: isotopomer price
param upper_bound: maximum proportion to test
---

methods.name: isodesign.base.isotopomer.Isotopomer.generate_fraction
documentation:
Generate numpy array containing list of possible fraction
between lower_bound and upper_bound depending of the step value.
Fractions will be used for influx_si simulations. Influx_si takes
only values that are between 0 and 1.

```
return

numpy array containing list of possible fraction
```
Class for the initiation of isotopomer

classes.name: isodesign.base.score.Score

methods.name: isodesign.base.score.Score.__init__
documentation:
---
param label_input: series containing fluxes SDs for a given label inputs
---

methods.name: isodesign.base.score.Score.__call__
documentation: Returns the score obtained after applying the criteria

methods.name: isodesign.base.score.Score.compute
documentation:
Computes the score and stores it in self.score according to the given criteria.

```
param criteria

the criteria to apply

param kwargs

additional arguments to pass to the selected criteria. If the weight is
```

not provided in kwargs for the selected criteria, the default value is 1.

methods.name: isodesign.base.score.Score.apply_sum_sd
documentation:
Returns the sum of standard deviations for a given label input

```
param weight_sum_sd

he weight to apply to the score
```

methods.name: isodesign.base.score.Score.apply_sum_nb_flux_sd
documentation:
Returns the total number of fluxes with sds below a given threshold.

```
param threshold

the threshold value used to filter the flux values

param weight_flux

the weight to apply to the score
```

methods.name: isodesign.base.score.Score.apply_number_labeled_inputs
documentation:
Returns the number of labelled substrates for each label input.

```
param info_linp_files_dict

dictionary containing namedtuples with
the number of labelled substrates for each label input

param weight_labeled_input

the weight to apply to the score
```

methods.name: isodesign.base.score.Score.apply_price
documentation:
Returns the price for each label input.

```
param info_linp_files_dict

dictionary containing namedtuples with
the price for each label input
```
Class containing the implementation of the rating criteria

classes.name: isodesign.base.score.ScoreHandler

methods.name: isodesign.base.score.ScoreHandler.__init__
documentation:
---
param dataframe: the summary dataframe (filtered or not) of the results of the various
  label input proportions following the simulation
---

methods.name: isodesign.base.score.ScoreHandler.apply_criteria
documentation:
Apply criteria to the dataframe columns via the score_object class

```
param criteria

method(s) to apply to the columns

param kwargs

additional arguments
```

methods.name: isodesign.base.score.ScoreHandler.apply_operations
documentation:
Apply an operation to the score_objects of the columns

```
param operation

the operation to apply to the score_objects (Addition, Multiply, Divide)
```

This class applies rating methods and performs operations between them.
.

functions.name: isodesign.ui.pages.3_Simulation_options.clear_text_input
documentation:
Clear the "add_options" widget (st.text_input 
widget) once the option has been added.
FUNCTIONS #

functions.name: isodesign.ui.pages.3_Simulation_options.delete_option
documentation: Delete an option from the list of added options.

functions.name: isodesign.ui.pages.3_Simulation_options.execute_simulation
documentation:
Execute a simulation task in a loop. Monitors for 
an external signal (via `st.session_state.running`) to terminate.

functions.name: isodesign.ui.pages.3_Simulation_options.start_simulation
documentation:
Launch the simulation task in a separate thread and 
wait for its completion. Ensures that the Streamlit 
runtime context is properly attached to the thread.

classes.name: isodesign.base.process.Process

methods.name: isodesign.base.process.Process.get_path_input_netw
documentation:
Get the directory path of the netw file (essential file containing
all reactions and transition labels). From this path, we also store
the directory path and the name of the model to be analyzed.

```
param netw_directory_path

str containing the path to the netw file
```

methods.name: isodesign.base.process.Process.load_model
documentation: Load MTF files depending on the model name.

methods.name: isodesign.base.process.Process.analyse_model
documentation:
Analyze model network to identify substrates, metabolites, etc by using 
modules from influx_si.

methods.name: isodesign.base.process.Process.copy_files
documentation:
Copy the imported files in the linp folder. 
All the files that will be passed to influx_si have to be in the
same folder.

methods.name: isodesign.base.process.Process.save_process_to_file
documentation: Save the Process object to a pickle file in the model directory.

methods.name: isodesign.base.process.Process.configure_unlabelled_form
documentation:
Add the unlabelled form of the inputs to the isotopomers dictionary 
(key: substrate name, value: list of isotopomers) with default values.

methods.name: isodesign.base.process.Process.add_isotopomer
documentation:
Add isotopomer to the isotopomers dictionary (self.isotopomers).

```
param substrate_name

name of the substrate

param labelling

labelling of the isotopomer

param intervals_nb

number of intervals to test

param lower_b

lower bound

param upper_b

upper bound

param price

price of the isotopomer.
```

methods.name: isodesign.base.process.Process.remove_isotopomer
documentation:
Remove isotopomer from the isotopomer dictionary (self.isotopomers)
according to the substrate name and labelling.

```
param substrate

substrate name

param labelling

labelling for isotopomer to remove
```

methods.name: isodesign.base.process.Process.generate_combinations
documentation:
Generate all possible combinations of labelled substrates 
using the LabelInput class.

methods.name: isodesign.base.process.Process.create_tmp_folder
documentation: Create a temp folder to store all the files generated by IsoDesign.

methods.name: isodesign.base.process.Process.clear_tmp_folder
documentation: Clear the temp folder containing all the files generated by IsoDesign.

methods.name: isodesign.base.process.Process.clear_previous_run
documentation: Clear the "_res" folder containing the results of the previous run.

methods.name: isodesign.base.process.Process.get_isotopomer_price
documentation:
Get the price of an isotopomer based on its labelling and name
from the isotopomers dictionary.

```
param isotopomer_labelling

isotopomer labelling

param isotopomer_name

isotopomer name
```

methods.name: isodesign.base.process.Process.configure_linp_files
documentation:
This method configures the structure and content of future 
linp files, a TSV format used for labelled simulations, 
grouping label input forms and their fractions. 
Dataframes are generated in linp format, each containing
a specific combination of labelled substrates. These dataframes 
are then stored and used to create the final linp files.

methods.name: isodesign.base.process.Process.remove_linp_configuration
documentation:
Removes linp DataFrames from the linp_dataframes
list according to the indices specified in the
list provided.

```
param index_to_remove

list of indices to remove
from linp_dataframes
```

methods.name: isodesign.base.process.Process.reintegrate_linp_configuration
documentation:
Reintegrates linp DataFrames into the linp_dataframes
list according to the indices specified in the
list provided.

```
param index_to_reintegrate

list of indices to reintegrate
into linp_dataframes.
```

methods.name: isodesign.base.process.Process.generate_linp_files
documentation:
Generates linp files (TSV format) in the temp folder (self.tmp_folder_path). 
Each file contains a combination of labelled substrates. 

A file is generated containing a mapping that associates each file 
number with its respective combinations.

methods.name: isodesign.base.process.Process.generate_vmtf_file
documentation:
Generate a vmtf file (TSV format) that permit to combine variable
and constant parts of a network model.
This file contained columns using imported files extensions. Each row contains
imported files names that will be used to produce a ftbl file used in the calculation. 
Each row have ftbl column with unique and non empty name.

methods.name: isodesign.base.process.Process.influx_simulation
documentation:
Run the simulation using the specified influx_si mode (stationary or instationary).

```
param param_list

List of command-line arguments to pass to influx_si.

param influx_mode

"influx_i" for instationary or "influx_s" for stationary mode.

return

A Popen object representing the running subprocess.
```

methods.name: isodesign.base.process.Process.generate_summary
documentation:
Read the tvar.sim files and generate a summary dataframe containing :
    - flux names, flux types,
    - the flux values in the tvar.sim files, 
    - the difference between the flux values in the input tvar file and the tvar.sim files,
    - flux SDs in each tvar.sim file

The summary dataframe is generated in an excel file in the results folder.
During the simulation, fluxes are added. These are highlighted in the summary dataframe.

methods.name: isodesign.base.process.Process.data_filter
documentation:
Filters summary dataframe by fluxes names, kind and/or metabolic pathway

```
param fluxes_names

list of fluxes names to be displayed

param kind

"NET", "XCH", "METAB"

param pathway

name of metabolic pathways to be displayed

return

filtered dataframe
```

methods.name: isodesign.base.process.Process.generate_score
documentation:
Generate a score for each labelled substrate combination according
on the criteria method(s) applied.

```
param method

list of criteria methods to apply

param operation

operation to apply to the scores (Addition, Multiply, Divide)

param kwargs

additional arguments for the rating methods
```

methods.name: isodesign.base.process.Process.draw_barplot
documentation:
Draw a bar plot with the scores dataframe.

```
param scores_to_plot

dataframe containing the scores to plot
```

methods.name: isodesign.base.process.Process.apply_log
documentation:
Apply a log of 10 to the values contained in 
the score table (stored in the self.scores attribute).

methods.name: isodesign.base.process.Process.register_scores
documentation:
Stores the analysis data (scores, the dataframe (filtered or not),
and the associated plot) in a dictionary for easy retrieval and organization.
This method updates the 'all_scores' dictionary with a new key
identified by the specified 'number'.

```
param number

Unique identifier for the analysis,
```

used to label each dictionary key(e.g., 'score_1', 'score_2', etc.)

methods.name: isodesign.base.process.Process.export_data
documentation:
Export the results of the analysis to an Excel file and an image file.

```
param score_name

Name of the score (ie. analysis) to be exported
```

The Process class is the main class to organise IsoDesign functionalities.
Key features:
- importing and reading data files
- generating combinations of isotopomers that are stored in influx_si .linp files
- runs simulations with influx_s to get predicted fluxes and associated SDs
- generates a summary.

functions.name: isodesign.__main__.get_last_version
documentation: Get last IsoDesign version.

functions.name: isodesign.__main__.main
documentation: The main routine

functions.name: isodesign.ui.pages.4_Results.register_multiselect_filter

FUNCTIONS #

functions.name: isodesign.ui.pages.4_Results.display_dataframe
documentation:
This function displays the dataframe showing the results of the simulations.
It also provides the logic for filtering this dataframe.

```
param count

number of the score block
```

functions.name: isodesign.ui.pages.4_Results.criteria_block
documentation:
This function displays the various criteria
and associated results once applied (in the
form of a score table and barplot).

```
param count

```

functions.name: isodesign.ui.pages.4_Results.new_scores
documentation:
This function generates a new score block. 
It also provides the logic for exporting the data.

functions.name: isodesign.ui.pages.2_Labels_input.get_carbon_length
documentation:
Obtain the carbon number of the substrate.
This number is retrieved from the netan dictionary (Process class).

```
param substrate

substrate name
```
FUNCTIONS #

functions.name: isodesign.ui.pages.2_Labels_input.remove_rows
documentation:
Function for deleting undesired rows (i.e. linp file configuration) 
from the linp overview dataframe according to the indexes 
selected by the user.

functions.name: isodesign.ui.pages.2_Labels_input.reintegrate_rows
documentation:
Function for reintegrating undisired rows (i.e. linp file configuration)
from the linp overview dataframe according to the indexes
selected by the user.

```
param indexes

list of indexes selected by the user
```

functions.name: isodesign.ui.Upload_data.get_path_netw
documentation: Open a file dialog to select a network file.
FUNCTIONS #

functions.name: isodesign.ui.Upload_data.logger_setup
documentation:
Set up a logger for the application. This method creates a logging handler
that writes logs to a file and a stream handler to the console.
The log level is set to INFO by default. If debug_mode is set to True,
the log level is set to DEBUG.

```
param output_path

the path where the log file will be saved

param debug_mode

if True, the logger will be set to debug mode.

return

the configured logger
```

functions.name: isodesign.ui.Upload_data.change_output_folder_path
documentation: Change the output folder path.
